place: Canaseraga village, New York, New York

facts.text: Canaseraga’s Henry Carter and Son foundry, founded in 1873, later became the Canisteo Steam Power Company and produced steam engines for local industry.
When the Erie Railroad arrived in 1851, Canaseraga swelled with new factories, schools, churches, and entertainment, turning the village into a bustling hub.
An 1873 map shows Canaseraga hosting three physicians, a dentist, four attorneys, a tailor, a cooper, a foundry, and several small manufacturers.
The Canisteo Silk Mill on Russell Street employed 200 workers in the 1920s, making it the only silk factory of its kind in the United States.
